root_cause.text: An integer overflow occurs in the `MDC2_Update` function when adding the current buffer length and the input length (`i + len`), causing large inputs to be incorrectly treated as partial blocks and leading to an out-of-bounds write during `memcpy`.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces the vulnerability-inducing addition check `if (i + len < MDC2_BLOCK)` with a safe subtraction check `if (len < MDC2_BLOCK - i)` to prevent the integer overflow.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: An integer overflow occurs in the `MDC2_Update` function when adding the current buffer length and the input length (`i + len`), causing large inputs to be incorrectly treated as partial blocks and leading to an out-of-bounds write during `memcpy`.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces the vulnerability-inducing addition check `if (i + len < MDC2_BLOCK)` with a safe subtraction check `if (len < MDC2_BLOCK - i)` to prevent the integer overflow.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix a potential integer overflow vulnerability in the block size bounds check.
intent_record.what: Changed the arithmetic condition from 'i + len < MDC2_BLOCK' to 'len < MDC2_BLOCK - i'.
intent_record.how: Prevents a potential integer overflow when adding the incoming length to the existing buffer size, ensuring the bounds check correctly catches oversized inputs.

grouping_result.intent: Fix a potential integer overflow vulnerability in the block size bounds check.

relevance_review.reason: The group's core intent directly addresses the integer overflow vulnerability in the bounds check, matching the vulnerability root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line contains the integer overflow (i + len) mentioned in the root cause, which incorrectly evaluates large inputs as partial blocks.
This line performs the memcpy which leads to the out-of-bounds write as a consequence of the integer overflow.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement contains the integer overflow (i + len) which incorrectly evaluates large inputs as partial blocks, matching the root cause.
This is the out-of-bounds write (memcpy) that occurs as a consequence of the integer overflow in the block size bounds check.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 0f113f3ee4d629ef9a4a30911b22b224772085e5
Author: Matt Caswell <matt@openssl.org>
Date:   Thu Jan 22 03:40:55 2015 +0000

    Run util/openssl-format-source -v -c .
    
    Reviewed-by: Tim Hudson <tjh@openssl.org>

diff --git a/crypto/mdc2/mdc2dgst.c b/crypto/mdc2/mdc2dgst.c
index 302f9ab9a8..17b994e95b 100644
--- a/crypto/mdc2/mdc2dgst.c
+++ b/crypto/mdc2/mdc2dgst.c
@@ -5,21 +5,21 @@
  * This package is an SSL implementation written
  * by Eric Young (eay@cryptsoft.com).
  * The impleme
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `MDC2_Update` contains the vulnerable condition `if (i + len < MDC2_BLOCK)` which is susceptible to an integer overflow if `len` is very large. This overflow would cause the code to incorrectly enter the partial block branch and perform a buffer overflow via `memcpy(&(c->data[i]), in, len);`. The commit only changes formatting and does not fix the logic.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Import of old SSLeay release: SSLeay 0.8.1b
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit d02b48c63a58ea4367a0e905979f140b7d090f86
Author: Ralf S. Engelschall <rse@openssl.org>
Date:   Mon Dec 21 10:52:47 1998 +0000

    Import of old SSLeay release: SSLeay 0.8.1b

diff --git a/crypto/mdc2/mdc2dgst.c b/crypto/mdc2/mdc2dgst.c
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..f4dfcf9bdc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/crypto/mdc2/mdc2dgst.c
@@ -0,0 +1,211 @@
+/* crypto/mdc2/mdc2dgst.c */
+/* Copyright (C) 1995-1997 Eric Young (eay@cryptsoft.com)
+ * All rights reserved.
+ *
+ * This package is an SSL
bic_trace_step.reason: The MDC2_Update function contains the vulnerable construct `if (i+len < MDC2_BLOCK)`. Since `i` is an `int` and `len` is an `unsigned long`, a sufficiently large `len` can cause the addition to overflow, resulting in a small value that bypasses the block size check and leads to a massive out-of-bounds `memcpy(&(c->data[i]),in,(int)len)`.

bic_trace_step.reason: The function `MDC2_Update` contains the vulnerable construct `if (i + len < MDC2_BLOCK)`. The addition `i + len` can overflow if `len` is very large, causing it to incorrectly evaluate as true. This leads to the `memcpy(&(c->data[i]), in, len);` execution, which will attempt to write out-of-bounds. The commit only applies formatting and does not fix the overflow.
